# Tasks — 2026-06-01

**Input**: `.github/research/2026-06-01-plan.md`
**Source research**: `.github/research/2026-06-01-research.md`
**Tracking issues**:

- **#632** — `[tool] Extend per-directory AGENTS.md system-reminders to write/edit/multiedit/grep` (Plan §A)
- **#633** — `[plugin] Plugin rule contributions — command source with timeout/size bounds and caching` (Plan §B, depends on `#595`)

## Scope

Two independent feature tracks are in scope this cycle. Both are sized **small
(< 4h)** in the plan. Track B is **blocked on `#595`** (rule-contribution
schema + inline/file sources) and MUST NOT begin until that issue lands; Track
A has no external blockers and can start immediately.

Items already covered in `src/` (#2 MCP env vars, #5 system-`rg` fast-path,
POSIX `cd`/`pushd` audit) and items still represented by an existing open
issue (#594 workdir filter, #595 rule schema) are explicitly **out of scope**
for this tasks file — see plan §"Items deliberately NOT filed this cycle".

All file paths below are repo-relative.

## Format: `[ID] [P?] Description`

- **[P]** = can be implemented in parallel with other `[P]` tasks (different
  files, no shared mutable state).
- Sequential (no `[P]`) tasks must land in the listed order — they share
  files or depend on a refactor made earlier in the list.
- Each task lists the **exact files** it touches and is small enough to be
  one reviewable commit.
- Tracks **A** and **B** are entirely independent; tasks within a track are
  ordered, but A* and B* tasks do not block each other (B is gated on a
  separate external issue, not on A).

---

## Track A — Per-directory AGENTS.md reminders for write/edit/multiedit/grep (#632)

### Phase A1: Extract the shared helper (refactor, no behaviour change)

**Purpose**: `attachAgentsMdReminders` currently lives as a private function
in `src/tool/tools/read.ts:55-76`. Move it into a shared module so all four
new call sites consume the same implementation, and so the existing `read`
behaviour is provably unchanged.

- [ ] **A001** Create `src/agent/agentsMdReminders.ts` exporting a generic
  `attachAgentsMdReminders<T>(result: ToolResult<T>, absPath, context)` whose
  body is the literal port of `read.ts:55-76`. Keep the workdir-relative
  `source` mapping and the `result.metadata.systemReminders` shape exactly as
  today. Export a single named symbol — no default export.
- [ ] **A002** Refactor `src/tool/tools/read.ts`:
  - Delete the local `attachAgentsMdReminders` definition (lines ~55-76).
  - Import the new shared helper from `../../agent/agentsMdReminders.js`.
  - All existing call sites in `read.ts` keep their current arguments;
    `npm test -- src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts` must pass
    with zero diff to the test file.

**Checkpoint**: `npm run typecheck && npm test -- read.agentsmd` is green.
Helper now lives in `src/agent/`; no other tool consumes it yet.

---

### Phase A2: Wire helper into `write`, `edit`, `multiedit`

**Purpose**: After a successful write, attach reminders for any unseen
`AGENTS.md` between `workdir` and the written path. Mirror `read.ts` exactly
— same shape, same opt-in via `context.agentsMdSeen`.

- [ ] **A003** [P] Modify `src/tool/tools/write.ts`:
  - After the successful `fs.writeFile` and **before** the
    `gitManager.onFileChanged` notification, call
    `attachAgentsMdReminders(result, absPath, context)`.
  - When `context.agentsMdSeen` is `undefined`, the call is a no-op (the
    helper already guards on this) — direct-test usage of `write` stays
    unaffected.
- [ ] **A004** [P] Modify `src/tool/tools/edit.ts`:
  - Same pattern, after the successful `fs.writeFile` (~line 117) and
    before `gitManager.onFileChanged`.
- [ ] **A005** [P] Modify `src/tool/tools/multiedit.ts`:
  - Same pattern, after the atomic write completes. Pass the **target file
    path** (not each per-edit position). Multiedit writes one file per call,
    so a single `attachAgentsMdReminders` invocation per execute is correct.

**Checkpoint**: `npm run typecheck` passes; existing write/edit/multiedit
tests still green (no behavioural change unless `agentsMdSeen` is supplied).

---

### Phase A3: Wire helper into `grep`

**Purpose**: `grep` returns matches across many files in a single call. Emit
**at most one** reminder per unique parent directory walked, bounded by the
shared `agentsMdSeen` so a hot directory only fires once per session.

- [ ] **A006** Modify `src/tool/tools/grep.ts`:
  - When `context.agentsMdSeen` is provided, after the matches array is
    finalised, iterate the **unique parent directories** of all match paths.
    For each, call `attachAgentsMdReminders(result, <a-file-in-that-dir>,
    context)` — the helper itself already de-dupes against `agentsMdSeen`,
    so we never emit a duplicate.
  - When `agentsMdSeen` is missing, skip entirely (mirror `read.ts`).
  - Keep the existing system-`rg` fast-path untouched (`grep.ts:60,604`).

**Checkpoint**: `npm run typecheck && npm run lint` pass. Existing grep tests
green; new grep behaviour exercised in Phase A4.

---

### Phase A4: Tests for all four new call sites

**Purpose**: Mirror `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts` for each
new tool. All tests follow the project's `fs.mkdtemp` + `afterEach` cleanup
convention (see `AGENTS.md` §"Testing quirks").

- [ ] **A007** [P] New file
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/write.agentsmd.test.ts`. Cases:
  1. Writing `apps/api/src/foo.ts` with an existing `apps/api/AGENTS.md`
     populates `result.metadata.systemReminders` (workdir-relative
     `source`, exact file content).
  2. No `AGENTS.md` between workdir and target ⇒ no `systemReminders`.
  3. Two writes in the same `agentsMdSeen` set ⇒ second write does not
     re-emit the same reminder.
  4. `context.agentsMdSeen` undefined ⇒ tool succeeds with no reminder
     metadata (regression guard for direct-test usage).
- [ ] **A008** [P] New file
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/edit.agentsmd.test.ts`. Same four cases as A007
  but exercising the `edit` tool against an existing seed file.
- [ ] **A009** [P] New file
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/multiedit.agentsmd.test.ts`. Same four cases as
  A007 but exercising `multiedit` (multiple edits on a single target file).
- [ ] **A010** [P] New file
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/grep.agentsmd.test.ts`. Cases:
  1. Grep matches across `apps/api/src/*.ts` and `apps/web/src/*.ts` with
     an `AGENTS.md` in **only** `apps/api/` ⇒ exactly one reminder, with
     `source = "apps/api/AGENTS.md"`.
  2. `AGENTS.md` files in **both** `apps/api/` and `apps/web/` ⇒ exactly
     two reminders, one per directory, no duplicates.
  3. Second grep call with the same `agentsMdSeen` ⇒ no reminders (full
     de-dupe across calls).
  4. `context.agentsMdSeen` undefined ⇒ no reminder metadata.

**Checkpoint**: `npm test -- agentsmd` runs all five `*.agentsmd.test.ts`
files green. `npm run test:coverage` still clears the CI 40% lines floor on
the new code paths (helper + four call sites).

---

### Phase A5: Verification (Track A)

- [ ] **A011** Run the project's pre-push gate locally in CI order
  (matches `AGENTS.md`):
  `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run format:check && npm run test:coverage && npm run build`.
- [ ] **A012** Confirm `coverage/coverage-summary.json` lines coverage stays

- [ ] **A013** Open the PR; reference issue **#632**. Use a Conventional

  `commitlint.config.cjs` — recommended `feat(tools): …`. Do **not** use
  `feat(agents): …` (not in the enum); use `feat(agent): …` only if the
  diff is dominated by `src/agent/agentsMdReminders.ts`.

## Track B — Plugin rule contributions, `command` source (#633)

> ⚠ **Blocked on `#595`** (plugin rule schema + inline/file sources). Do not
> start B001 until #595 is merged: B's tasks all extend a schema and loader
> that #595 introduces. If #595 ships in a different shape than the plan
> assumes, revise this track before continuing.

### Phase B1: Rule-command runner (pure module, fully unit-tested first)

**Purpose**: Add the spawn helper as an exported pure-ish module with strict
bounds (timeout, stdout cap, no shell, secrets stripped). No callers wired
yet — this phase is a self-contained module.

- [ ] **B001** Create `src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts` exporting
  `runRuleCommand({ argv, cwd, env, timeoutMs, maxBytes }): Promise<{ stdout: string; truncated: boolean }>`.
  Behaviour:
  - Spawn via `child_process.execFile` (no shell). `argv[0]` is the binary,
    `argv.slice(1)` are arguments. Reject when `argv` is empty.
  - Enforce timeout via `AbortController` with default `timeoutMs = 5000`.
    On timeout: kill the child, reject with a typed error
    (`{ code: 'TIMEOUT' }`).
  - Cap stdout via a counting pipe at `maxBytes` (default 32 KB / 32 768
    bytes). On overflow: kill the child, return
    `{ stdout: <first 32 KB>, truncated: true }`.
  - `cwd` defaults to the plugin root (caller-supplied).
  - `env`: pass through caller-supplied env minus a denylist of known
    secret-bearing keys (`AICORE_*`, `SAP_PROXY_*`, anything matching
    `/SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|KEY/i` except `PATH`/`HOME`). Document the
    denylist inline.
  - Non-zero exit → reject with `{ code: 'EXIT', exitCode, stderr }`.
- [ ] **B002** [P] Unit tests in
  `src/plugin/__tests__/ruleCommandRunner.test.ts`. Cases:
  1. Successful command (`echo hello`-equivalent via a tiny inline node
     script) → stdout returned untruncated, `truncated: false`.
  2. Timeout: command sleeps past `timeoutMs` → rejects with `code: 'TIMEOUT'`.
  3. Oversized stdout: command emits > 32 KB → returns first 32 KB with
     `truncated: true` and the child is killed (no zombie).
  4. Non-zero exit → rejects with `code: 'EXIT'` and captured stderr.
  5. Empty `argv` → synchronous reject with a clear error.
  6. Secret denylist: env entries matching the denylist regex are dropped;
     `PATH` and `HOME` survive.

**Checkpoint**: `npm test -- ruleCommandRunner` green. No callers wired yet.

---

### Phase B2: Schema extension + cache layer

**Purpose**: Extend the `#595` schema to recognise `source: 'command'` plus
a `scope: 'session' | 'always'` field, and add a keyed cache.

- [ ] **B003** Extend the rule-contribution Zod schema in
  `src/plugin/index.ts` (location introduced by #595):
  - Add `source: z.literal('command')` as a new union variant.
  - For `command` rules, require `argv: z.array(z.string()).min(1)` and
    optional `scope: z.enum(['session', 'always']).default('session')`,
    `timeoutMs: z.number().int().positive().max(30_000).default(5_000)`.
  - Reject mixing `source: 'command'` with the inline/file fields from
    #595 at parse time (mutually exclusive).
- [ ] **B004** Add the cache. Two cache maps inside `src/plugin/index.ts`
  (or a tiny `src/plugin/ruleCache.ts` if it grows beyond a screen):
  - `alwaysCache: Map<string, string>` keyed by
    `${pluginId}::${ruleName}` — populated once per process lifetime.
  - `sessionCache: Map<string, Map<string, string>>` keyed by
    `sessionId → (${pluginId}::${ruleName} → content)`. Drop entries on
    session end (hook into `sessionManager` end-of-session signal if one
    exists; otherwise rely on process exit and document the limitation).
  - Provide a small `getOrLoad(scope, key, loader)` accessor used in B005.
- [ ] **B005** Wire the loader: when the schema parses a `command` rule,
  resolve the active session id via
  `sessionManager.getActiveSession()?.metadata.id` (fall back to a synthetic
  `'no-session'` id when none is active) and call `getOrLoad` →
  `runRuleCommand` from B001. On runner rejection: log via the existing
  plugin logger, **omit** that single rule, and continue loading the
  remaining rules (one bad command must not break the whole plugin).

**Checkpoint**: `npm run typecheck` passes; rule-contribution tests from
#595 still green; new schema variants accepted.

---

### Phase B3: System-prompt assembly + integration tests

- [ ] **B006** Extend `src/agent/system.ts` to consume command-sourced rule
  content alongside the inline/file sources from #595. Cache lookups happen
  in the loader (B004); `system.ts` only sees a flat
  `Array<{ source: string; content: string }>` so its surface area stays
  unchanged. Verify ordering: command rules interleave with file/inline
  rules in plugin-declared order.
- [ ] **B007** Integration tests in
  `src/plugin/__tests__/ruleCommand.test.ts`. Cases:
  1. **Happy path**: a fixture plugin declares a `command` rule; output
     reaches the assembled system prompt verbatim.
  2. **Timeout**: command exceeds `timeoutMs`; rule omitted, error logged,
     **other rules still load** (assert via a sibling inline rule that
     survives).
  3. **Oversized stdout**: > 32 KB output → truncated at 32 KB with a
     single trailing line noting truncation; system prompt receives the
     truncated content.
  4. **`scope: 'session'` cache**: same session id reuses the cached
     output (spawn called once across two loader invocations); a different
     session id triggers a re-spawn.
  5. **`scope: 'always'` cache**: subsequent calls (any session) do not
     re-spawn for the lifetime of the test process.
  6. **Secret denylist**: a `command` rule whose argv echoes
     `process.env.AICORE_SERVICE_KEY` returns empty (env stripped). Use
     a fake env value set via `vi.stubEnv` so we don't depend on real
     credentials (tests must not require live SAP creds — see `AGENTS.md`
     §"Environment").

**Checkpoint**: `npm test -- ruleCommand` green; coverage for
`src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts` and the new schema branch in
`src/plugin/index.ts` ≥ project floor.

---

### Phase B4: Verification (Track B)

- [ ] **B008** Run the local pre-push gate in CI order:
  `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run format:check && npm run test:coverage && npm run build`.
- [ ] **B009** Confirm `coverage/coverage-summary.json` lines ≥ 40%.
- [ ] **B010** Open the PR; reference **#633** and note the dependency on
  **#595** in the description (e.g. "Builds on #595 — merge #595 first").
  Use a Conventional Commits message — recommended scope is
  `feat(config): …` if the diff is schema-heavy, otherwise leave the scope
  off rather than invent one (allowed scopes are listed in
  `commitlint.config.cjs`).

## Dependency graph

```
Track A (#632 — independent):

A001 ──► A002 ──┬──► A003 [P] ──┐
                ├──► A004 [P] ──┤
                ├──► A005 [P] ──┤
                └──► A006      ─┤
                                 ├──► A007 [P] ──┐
                                 ├──► A008 [P] ──┤
                                 ├──► A009 [P] ──┼──► A011 → A012 → A013
                                 └──► A010 [P] ──┘

Track B (#633 — blocked on #595):

  #595 (external)
       │
       ▼
B001 ──► B002 [P] ──┐
                     ├──► B003 ──► B004 ──► B005 ──► B006 ──► B007 ──► B008 → B009 → B010
                     │
                     (B002 can run alongside B003 — different files)
```

- A001 must land before any A00{3..6} caller switches to the shared
  helper — they all import the new module path.
- A007–A010 are flagged `[P]` because each test file is independent of the
  others and only exercises one tool's call site.
- B001 and B002 can land in the same commit; B003 onward depends on B001
  being merged so the loader has a runner to call.
- The two tracks share **no files**, so Track A's PR and Track B's PR can
  open and merge in any order (A is also unblocked first since #595 still
  needs to ship).

Pulled from the plan's "Items deliberately NOT filed this cycle" — listed
here only so a future tasks file knows where to start, **not** as work to do
now:

- Auto-mode classifier, OTEL events, MessageDisplay hook, agents PR column,
  dynamic workflows, marketplace `defaultEnabled`, Cline Hub, Discord,
  mercury-2, OpenRouter, aider model-list, Opus-4.8 adaptive-thinking gate.
- Opus 4.8 SAP model id — pick up when SAP publishes the deployment id.
- Re-baselining against newer claude-code / kilocode releases.
